meta.name: HCL-32 Hypomania Checklist
meta.description: The HCL-32 is a 32-item self-report screening tool developed by Jules Angst et al. to identify hypomanic symptoms and help screen for bipolar spectrum disorders. Think back to a time when you were in a 'high' or 'excited' state.
meta.author: Angst et al. (2005)
meta.reference: Angst J, Adolfsson R, Benazzi F, et al. The HCL-32: Towards a self-assessment tool for hypomanic symptoms in outpatients. J Affect Disord. 2005;88(2):217-233.
meta.instruction: Think of a time when you were in a 'high' or 'excited' state. How did you feel then? Please answer Yes or No for each item.

questions.text: I need less sleep
I feel more energetic and more active
I am more self-confident
I enjoy my work more
I am more sociable (make more phone calls, go out more)
I want to travel and/or do travel more
I tend to drive faster or take more risks when driving
I spend more money / too much money
I take more risks in my daily life (in my work and/or other activities)
I am physically more active (sport etc.)
I plan more activities or projects
I have more ideas, I am more creative
I am less shy or inhibited
I wear more colorful and more extravagant clothes/make-up
I want to meet or actually do meet more people
I am more interested in sex, and/or have increased sexual desire
I am more flirtatious and/or am more sexually active
I talk more
I think faster
I make more jokes or puns when I am talking
I am more easily distracted
I engage in lots of new things
My thoughts jump from topic to topic
I do things more quickly and/or more easily
I am more impatient and/or get irritable more easily
I can be exhausting or irritating for others
I get into more quarrels
My mood is higher, more optimistic
I drink more coffee
I smoke more cigarettes
I drink more alcohol
I take more drugs (sedatives, anti-anxiety pills, stimulants)

ranges.label: Few Hypomanic Features
ranges.description: You reported few hypomanic symptoms, below the screening threshold. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: No particular concern at this time
If you experience mood fluctuations, consider consulting a professional
Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities

ranges.label: Some Hypomanic Features
ranges.description: You reported some hypomanic symptoms, but below the screening threshold (14). Consider monitoring mood changes. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Pay attention to patterns and duration of mood fluctuations
Keeping a mood diary can help with self-understanding
If mood swings affect daily life, consider consulting a professional
Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques

ranges.label: Screen Positive
ranges.description: Your score meets or exceeds the standard screening threshold (>=14), suggesting possible bipolar spectrum features. This is not a diagnosis and requires professional evaluation.
suggestions: Consider consulting a psychiatrist for further evaluation
Keep detailed records of mood patterns for your appointment
Learn about bipolar spectrum disorders
Do not self-diagnose; respect professional assessment
Consider consulting a mental health professional

ranges.label: Prominent Hypomanic Features
ranges.description: You reported many hypomanic symptoms. A comprehensive psychiatric evaluation is strongly recommended. Your mental health concerns are notable and may be impacting daily functioning. Speaking with a mental health professional is recommended.
suggestions: Strongly recommended to see a psychiatrist soon
Document patterns of mood elevation and depression
Provide your doctor with a complete mood history
If currently on medication, do not adjust dosage on your own
Schedule a mental health professional assessment as soon as possible

active_elated.label: Low
active_elated.description: Low score on the Active/Elated dimension. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

active_elated.label: Moderate
active_elated.description: Some active/elated features present. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

active_elated.label: High
active_elated.description: Prominent active/elated features such as increased energy, confidence, and sociability. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions

risk_irritable.label: Low
risk_irritable.description: Low score on the Risk-Taking/Irritable dimension. This indicates that your overall mental health is in good condition, with all areas of functioning working well.
suggestions: Continue maintaining a healthy lifestyle and mental well-being
Focus on personal growth and positive experiences
Maintain regular exercise and social activities
Remember to seek support when facing difficulties

risk_irritable.label: Moderate
risk_irritable.description: Some risk-taking or irritability tendencies. Mild psychological concerns are common in life. Focusing on self-care and healthy coping strategies can help maintain well-being.
suggestions: Focus on self-care with good routines and regular exercise
Learn simple relaxation and stress-relief techniques
Stay in communication with people you trust
If symptoms persist or worsen, consider consulting a professional
Maintain a positive outlook on life

risk_irritable.label: High
risk_irritable.description: Prominent risk-taking/irritable features such as impulsive spending, distractibility, and irritability. At this level, psychological concerns may be affecting your daily life. It is recommended to take active steps and consider available support options.
suggestions: Consider consulting a mental health professional
Identify your specific sources of concern
Maintain a structured daily routine
Try relaxation and coping methods that suit you
Engage in meaningful activities and social interactions
